---
name: content-research-writer
title: Content Research Writer
description: "Use when writing blog posts, articles, newsletters, or tutorials that need research, citations, structured outlines, and iterative feedback."
---

## content-research-writer

## Verification Checklist

- [ ] Environment and dependencies are properly configured
- [ ] Content Research Writer output was generated successfully
- [ ] Output meets expected quality and style requirements
- [ ] Any errors during execution were resolved
- [ ] Final result is saved or delivered as expected

## Description

Collaborative writing partner for research-assisted content creation. Helps structure outlines, conduct research with proper citations, improve hooks, and provide iterative feedback while preserving unique voice.

## When to Use

- Writing blog posts, articles, or newsletters
- Creating tutorials or thought leadership pieces
- Drafting case studies or research papers
- Technical documentation with research requirements
- Content that needs citations and sources
- Iterative writing with feedback loops

## When NOT to Use

- Quick social media posts or short messages
- Code documentation (use code-docs instead)
- Internal memos without research needs
- Real-time collaborative editing

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Structure & Outline

- Discuss topic and target audience
- Create outline with key sections
- Identify research needs

### Phase 2: Research & Gather

- Conduct web searches for sources
- Collect citations and references
- Organize findings by section

### Phase 3: Draft & Iterate

- Write sections with research integrated
- Provide feedback on clarity and flow
- Refine hooks and transitions

### Phase 4: Polish & Finalize

- Review for consistency and voice
- Verify all citations
- Final proofreading

## Tools & References

- **Related Skills**: writing-clearly-and-concisely, humanizer
- **Citation Formats**: APA, MLA, Chicago
- **Research Tools**: Web search, academic databases

## Best Practices

- Preserve author's unique voice throughout
- Use proper citation format consistently
- Include sources for all claims
- Get feedback early and often
- Organize research before writing
- Document sources as you go
